title: leucocarpus perfoliatus
intro: Leucocarpus perfoliatus, also known as the Buttonbush or Seaside Bottlebrush, is a type of flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leucocarpus perfoliatus include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Leucocarpus perfoliatus can be made by infusing its flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by drying the flowers and using them in a decoction or infusion.
